hypothesis: If single-step retrosynthesis models are trained on randomly split USPTO-retro data enriched in high-frequency templates and structurally similar products, then their top-k and round-trip accuracies will be inflated and correlate with template frequency and similarity yet drop sharply on Pistachio OOD—more so for the template-based model than for template-free models—because random splits induce dataset bias that favors template memorization over transferable chemical reasoning.
